action.reasoning: Observation: MetricGAN+ achieves val PESQ=2.862 (test=3.137 best), ConvTasNet after 10+ epochs only ~2.007 val PESQ with norm (train loss improved to -14.2 but insufficient). test_enhance.py ran successfully with safe_globals fix, confirms flat postproc. train.py val loop appears truncated/incomplete in workspace (PESQ calc missing loudness norm). Analysis: Sequential hybrid (ConvTasNet as pre-filter for residual noise removal then MetricGAN+) can be complementary since each targets different aspects (ConvTasNet for time-freq masking, MetricGAN for perceptual PESQ opt); low-risk test on 100 val samples. Fixing train.py fully (complete val with norm+PESQ, better logging to metrics.jsonl, more epochs, lower LR) enables continued improvement. Root cause of low ConvTasNet PESQ: undertraining + no norm in val + possible loss imbalance. Decision: Rewrite test_enhance.py (clean, add hybrid func + PESQ eval), run to compare 3 approaches (if hybrid >2.862 adopt). Update train.py (fix/complete val loop with loudness norm + PESQ, proper per-epoch metrics.jsonl, continue from best.pt, 30 epochs). Submit_eval only if hybrid wins; else restore MetricGAN baseline.
